name: FAL Video Generation
description: Generate cinematic AI videos using FAL.ai (Veo3, Kling 2.1, Hailuo-02, MiniMax). Supports text-to-video and image-to-video with seamless looping for ASMR / ambient backgrounds.

required_variables.description: FAL.ai API key (get from https://fal.ai/dashboard/keys). Falls back to FAL_KEY if set.

skill.name: fal_video
skill.description: Generate cinematic AI videos using FAL.ai (Veo3, Kling 2.1, Hailuo-02). Supports text-to-video and image-to-video, plus seamless loop creation via FFmpeg.
skill.system_prompt:
You have access to FAL.ai video generation tools.

## Available models

**Image-to-video** (most useful for ASMR/ambient loops — start with a still and add gentle motion):
- `fal-ai/kling-video/v2.1/pro/image-to-video` — Kling 2.1 Pro. High quality, good motion control. Duration: 5 or 10s.
- `fal-ai/minimax/hailuo-02/standard/image-to-video` — Hailuo-02 Standard. Fast, cheap, great for subtle ambient motion. Duration: 6 or 10s.
- `fal-ai/minimax/hailuo-02/pro/image-to-video` — Hailuo-02 Pro. Higher fidelity. Duration: 6 or 10s.
- `fal-ai/veo3/image-to-video` — Veo3. Cinematic. Slowest and most expensive but highest quality. Duration: 4 or 8s.

**Text-to-video**:
- `fal-ai/veo3` — Veo3 text-to-video.
- `fal-ai/kling-video/v2.1/pro/text-to-video` — Kling 2.1 Pro text-to-video.

## Workflow

### For ASMR / ambient backgrounds (image-to-video → seamless loop)
1. Generate or download a still image (e.g. cozy fireplace office, snow falling outside window). Use Nano Banana via fal_image skill.
2. Call `image_to_video` with a SUBTLE motion prompt. Two flavours:
   - **Loopable motion** (fire flicker, candle flame, ember glow, abstract light shifts, mist drift in place) → either crossfade or palindrome looks fine.
   - **Directional motion** (rain falling, snow falling, waterfall, river flowing, clouds drifting one way) → the source MUST be self-loopable so a short crossfade at the seam is invisible. Tell the model to keep the motion **continuous and uniform** across the whole clip with **no acceleration or deceleration**, and keep camera STATIC.
   Examples of phrasing that crossfades cleanly:
     - "Continuous gentle rain falling at a steady rate from top to bottom of frame, identical density and speed throughout, no gusts, no thunder, static camera"
     - "Steady waterfall flow at a constant rate, identical density and speed throughout, no surge, no splash variation, static camera"
   Avoid: "rain starting softly and building", "snow swirling in gusts", "camera slowly zooming" — these all break crossfade looping.
3. Recommended models for this use case:
   - `fal-ai/minimax/hailuo-02/standard/image-to-video` (cheapest)
   - `fal-ai/kling-video/v2.1/pro/image-to-video` (best quality)
4. Once you have the clip, call `make_seamless_loop` with the desired total duration (e.g. 1800s for a 30-min video).
   - Default `loop_method` is **`crossfade`** — plays the source forward and crossfades each seam. Use this for **rain, snow, waterfalls, rivers, clouds** — any directional motion. Tune `crossfade_seconds` (default 1.0) up to 1.5–2.0 if motion is slow.
   - Use `loop_method: "palindrome"` only for symmetric/abstract motion (fire flicker, candle flame, generic mist) where reversing the second half is invisible. NEVER use palindrome on rain/snow/waterfall — it will run upward / backwards.

### For short cinematic clips (text-to-video)
1. Call `text_to_video` with a detailed prompt (camera, lighting, mood, motion).
2. Each clip is 4-10 seconds. Stitch via `stitch_clips` if needed.

## Aspect ratios
- 16:9 horizontal: `aspect_ratio: "16:9"`
- 9:16 vertical (Shorts/TikTok): `aspect_ratio: "9:16"`
- 1:1 square: `aspect_ratio: "1:1"`

## Tips
- For ASMR ambient: prefer image-to-video with SUBTLE motion. Big camera moves break the loop illusion.
- All `image_to_video` and `text_to_video` calls poll until completion — internal timeout is 10 min. They return a local mp4 path (already downloaded) plus the FAL CDN URL.
- Use `check_task` if a job's request_id is given but the polling timed out.

tools.name: image_to_video
tools.description: Generate a video from a source image and motion prompt using FAL.ai. Polls until complete (up to 10 min) and downloads the result locally. Default model is Hailuo-02 Standard (cheap, good for ASMR/ambient loops).

image_url.description: Public HTTPS URL of the source image (FAL.ai must be able to fetch it). The Nano Banana CDN URL works directly.

prompt.description: Motion/animation prompt. For ASMR/ambient: 'gentle fire flicker, soft particle drift, very slow drift'. Keep it specific and short (under 200 chars).

duration.description: Clip duration. Hailuo: '6' or '10'. Kling: '5' or '10'. Veo3: '4' or '8'. Default: '6'.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
API_KEY="${VAR_FAL_API_KEY:-${VAR_FAL_KEY:-}}"
if [ -z "$API_KEY" ]; then
  echo '{"error":"FAL API key not configured (set fal_api_key or FAL_KEY)"}'; exit 0
fi
MODEL="${ARG_MODEL:-fal-ai/minimax/hailuo-02/standard/image-to-video}"
IMG="$ARG_IMAGE_URL"
PROMPT="$ARG_PROMPT"
DUR="${ARG_DURATION:-6}"
AR="${ARG_ASPECT_RATIO:-16:9}"
WORK_DIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}"; mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
UUID=$(python3 -c 'import uuid;print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:10])')
OUT="$WORK_DIR/fal-i2v-$UUID.mp4"

PAYLOAD=$(python3 -c "
import json,sys
p={'prompt':sys.argv[1],'image_url':sys.argv[2],'duration':sys.argv[3],'aspect_ratio':sys.argv[4]}
print(json.dumps(p))
" "$PROMPT" "$IMG" "$DUR" "$AR")

SUBMIT=$(curl -sS --max-time 30 -X POST "https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL" \
  -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "$PAYLOAD")

REQ_ID=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('request_id',''))
except: pass")
STATUS_URL=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('status_url',''))
except: pass")
RESP_URL=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('response_url',''))
except: pass")

if [ -z "$REQ_ID" ]; then
  ERR=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | head -c 800 | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))')
  echo "{\"error\":\"FAL submit failed\",\"raw\":$ERR}"; exit 0
fi

[ -z "$STATUS_URL" ] && STATUS_URL="https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL/requests/$REQ_ID/status"
[ -z "$RESP_URL" ] && RESP_URL="https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL/requests/$REQ_ID"

# Poll up to 10 minutes (60 attempts * 10s)
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  sleep 10
  ST=$(curl -sS --max-time 15 -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" "$STATUS_URL")
  STATUS=$(echo "$ST" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('status',''))
except: pass")
  if [ "$STATUS" = "COMPLETED" ]; then
    RR=$(curl -sS --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" "$RESP_URL")
    VURL=$(echo "$RR" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin)
    v=d.get('video') or {}
    print(v.get('url',''))
except: pass")
    if [ -z "$VURL" ]; then
      echo "{\"error\":\"FAL returned no video URL\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\"}"; exit 0
    fi
    curl -sSL -o "$OUT" "$VURL"
    SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$OUT")
    DURM=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    echo "{\"video_url\":\"$VURL\",\"local_file\":\"$OUT\",\"size_bytes\":$SIZE,\"duration\":\"$DURM\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\",\"model\":\"$MODEL\"}"
    exit 0
  fi
  if [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "ERROR" ]; then
    echo "{\"error\":\"FAL job failed\",\"status\":\"$STATUS\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\"}"; exit 0
  fi
done
echo "{\"error\":\"FAL job timed out\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\",\"status_url\":\"$STATUS_URL\",\"response_url\":\"$RESP_URL\",\"hint\":\"Use check_task with this request_id and model.\"}"

tools.name: text_to_video
tools.description: Generate a video from a text prompt using FAL.ai. Polls until complete (up to 10 min) and downloads the result locally.

prompt.description: Text description of the video. Be specific about subject, camera, lighting, mood, motion.

duration.description: Duration string. Veo3: '4' or '8'. Kling: '5' or '10'.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
API_KEY="${VAR_FAL_API_KEY:-${VAR_FAL_KEY:-}}"
if [ -z "$API_KEY" ]; then
  echo '{"error":"FAL API key not configured"}'; exit 0
fi
MODEL="${ARG_MODEL:-fal-ai/veo3}"
PROMPT="$ARG_PROMPT"
DUR="${ARG_DURATION:-}"
AR="${ARG_ASPECT_RATIO:-16:9}"
WORK_DIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}"; mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
UUID=$(python3 -c 'import uuid;print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:10])')
OUT="$WORK_DIR/fal-t2v-$UUID.mp4"

PAYLOAD=$(python3 -c "
import json,sys
p={'prompt':sys.argv[1],'aspect_ratio':sys.argv[2]}
if sys.argv[3]: p['duration']=sys.argv[3]
print(json.dumps(p))
" "$PROMPT" "$AR" "$DUR")

SUBMIT=$(curl -sS --max-time 30 -X POST "https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL" -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$PAYLOAD")
REQ_ID=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('request_id',''))
except: pass")
if [ -z "$REQ_ID" ]; then
  ERR=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | head -c 800 | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))')
  echo "{\"error\":\"FAL submit failed\",\"raw\":$ERR}"; exit 0
fi
STATUS_URL=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('status_url',''))
except: pass")
RESP_URL=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('response_url',''))
except: pass")
[ -z "$STATUS_URL" ] && STATUS_URL="https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL/requests/$REQ_ID/status"
[ -z "$RESP_URL" ] && RESP_URL="https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL/requests/$REQ_ID"
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  sleep 10
  ST=$(curl -sS --max-time 15 -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" "$STATUS_URL")
  STATUS=$(echo "$ST" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('status',''))
except: pass")
  if [ "$STATUS" = "COMPLETED" ]; then
    RR=$(curl -sS --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" "$RESP_URL")
    VURL=$(echo "$RR" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin)
    v=d.get('video') or {}
    print(v.get('url',''))
except: pass")
    if [ -z "$VURL" ]; then echo "{\"error\":\"no video url\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\"}"; exit 0; fi
    curl -sSL -o "$OUT" "$VURL"
    SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$OUT")
    DURM=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    echo "{\"video_url\":\"$VURL\",\"local_file\":\"$OUT\",\"size_bytes\":$SIZE,\"duration\":\"$DURM\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\",\"model\":\"$MODEL\"}"
    exit 0
  fi
  if [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "ERROR" ]; then
    echo "{\"error\":\"FAL job failed\",\"status\":\"$STATUS\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\"}"; exit 0
  fi
done
echo "{\"error\":\"timeout\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\"}"

tools.name: check_task
tools.description: Check status of a FAL.ai video task without polling. Useful for jobs that timed out.

model.description: The FAL model id used to submit.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
API_KEY="${VAR_FAL_API_KEY:-${VAR_FAL_KEY:-}}"
if [ -z "$API_KEY" ]; then echo '{"error":"FAL API key not configured"}'; exit 0; fi
REQ="$ARG_REQUEST_ID"; MODEL="$ARG_MODEL"

# Build a list of candidate parent-model paths to try, since FAL truncates
# routing to the parent (e.g. fal-ai/minimax/hailuo-02/standard/image-to-video
# → status URL uses fal-ai/minimax). We try the full path first, then walk up.
CANDIDATES=()
PATH_TRY="$MODEL"
while [ -n "$PATH_TRY" ]; do
  CANDIDATES+=("$PATH_TRY")
  # Strip last segment
  if echo "$PATH_TRY" | grep -q '/'; then
    PATH_TRY=$(echo "$PATH_TRY" | sed 's|/[^/]*$||')
    # Stop after we've stripped down to one segment (e.g. "fal-ai")
    if ! echo "$PATH_TRY" | grep -q '/'; then break; fi
  else
    break
  fi
done

FOUND_STATUS=""
FOUND_VURL=""
WORKING_PATH=""
for P in "${CANDIDATES[@]}"; do
  ST=$(curl -sS --max-time 15 -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" "https://queue.fal.run/$P/requests/$REQ/status")
  STATUS=$(echo "$ST" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('status',''))
except: pass")
  if [ -n "$STATUS" ]; then
    FOUND_STATUS="$STATUS"
    WORKING_PATH="$P"
    break
  fi
done

if [ -z "$FOUND_STATUS" ]; then
  echo "{\"status\":\"\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ\",\"error\":\"could not find task at any FAL parent path\",\"tried\":\"${CANDIDATES[*]}\"}"
  exit 0
fi

if [ "$FOUND_STATUS" = "COMPLETED" ]; then
  RR=$(curl -sS --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" "https://queue.fal.run/$WORKING_PATH/requests/$REQ")
  FOUND_VURL=$(echo "$RR" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); v=d.get('video') or {}; print(v.get('url',''))
except: pass")
fi

if [ -n "$FOUND_VURL" ]; then
  echo "{\"status\":\"$FOUND_STATUS\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ\",\"video_url\":\"$FOUND_VURL\",\"resolved_path\":\"$WORKING_PATH\"}"
else
  echo "{\"status\":\"$FOUND_STATUS\",\"request_id\":\"$REQ\",\"resolved_path\":\"$WORKING_PATH\"}"
fi

tools.name: download_video
tools.description: Download a video URL to AT_WORK_DIR and return the local path.
tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
VIDEO_URL="$ARG_VIDEO_URL"
FILENAME="$ARG_FILENAME"
WORK_DIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}"; mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
if [ -z "$FILENAME" ]; then
  FILENAME="fal-$(python3 -c 'import uuid;print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:10])').mp4"
fi
OUT="$WORK_DIR/$FILENAME"
curl -sSL -o "$OUT" "$VIDEO_URL"
if [ ! -s "$OUT" ]; then echo '{"error":"download failed"}'; exit 0; fi
SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$OUT")
DUR=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
echo "{\"file\":\"$OUT\",\"size_bytes\":$SIZE,\"duration\":\"$DUR\"}"

tools.name: make_seamless_loop
tools.description:
Take a short video clip and produce a longer seamlessly-looped video. Two methods:
- 'crossfade' (DEFAULT, recommended for ASMR with directional motion like rain/snow/waterfall): plays source forward N times and crossfades each seam with FFmpeg xfade, so motion direction is always preserved.
- 'palindrome' (legacy): forward+reverse concatenation. Frame-perfect seamless but reverses motion direction in every other half — DO NOT USE for rain, snow, waterfalls, rivers, or anything that should keep falling/flowing one way.

input_video.description: Local path or URL of the source clip.

loop_method.description: 'crossfade' (default, forward-only with xfade seams — preserves motion direction) or 'palindrome' (forward+reverse — reverses directional motion). Use 'crossfade' for rain/snow/waterfall/river. Palindrome only for symmetric motion like fire flicker.

crossfade_seconds.description: Length of each crossfade transition in seconds. Default 1.0. Increase to 1.5–2.0 for slower motion. Used only when loop_method='crossfade'.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
IN="$ARG_INPUT_VIDEO"; TARGET="${ARG_TARGET_SECONDS:-60}"; MUTE="${ARG_MUTE:-true}"
METHOD="${ARG_LOOP_METHOD:-crossfade}"
XFADE="${ARG_CROSSFADE_SECONDS:-1.0}"
WORK_DIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}"; mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
SRC="$IN"
if echo "$IN" | grep -qE '^https?://'; then
  SRC="$WORK_DIR/loop-src-$(python3 -c 'import uuid;print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:8])').mp4"
  curl -sSL -o "$SRC" "$IN"
fi
if [ ! -f "$SRC" ]; then echo '{"error":"source not found"}'; exit 0; fi
DUR=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$SRC")
UUID=$(python3 -c 'import uuid;print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:8])')

if [ "$METHOD" = "palindrome" ]; then
  REV="$WORK_DIR/loop-rev-$UUID.mp4"
  ffmpeg -y -i "$SRC" -vf reverse -af areverse -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -c:a aac "$REV" 2>/dev/null
  UNIT="$WORK_DIR/loop-unit-$UUID.mp4"
  cat > "$WORK_DIR/concat-pal-$UUID.txt" <<EOF
file '$SRC'
file '$REV'
EOF
  ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$WORK_DIR/concat-pal-$UUID.txt" -c copy "$UNIT" 2>/dev/null
  UDUR=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$UNIT")
  TIMES=$(python3 -c "import math;print(int(math.ceil(${TARGET}/${UDUR})))")
  : > "$WORK_DIR/concat-loop-$UUID.txt"
  for i in $(seq 1 $TIMES); do echo "file '$UNIT'" >> "$WORK_DIR/concat-loop-$UUID.txt"; done
  LOOPED="$WORK_DIR/loop-full-$UUID.mp4"
  ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$WORK_DIR/concat-loop-$UUID.txt" -t "$TARGET" -c copy "$LOOPED" 2>/dev/null || \
    ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$WORK_DIR/concat-loop-$UUID.txt" -t "$TARGET" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -c:a aac "$LOOPED" 2>/dev/null
  FINAL_RAW="$LOOPED"
  REPEATS=$TIMES
  EXTRA_INFO="\"unit_duration\":$UDUR,\"method\":\"palindrome\""
else
  # crossfade method — build a self-loopable unit using head/tail xfade,
  # then stream_loop it to reach the target. Motion stays directional.
  #
  # Construction (D = source duration, X = crossfade seconds):
  #   middle = source[X .. D-X]                         length D-2X
  #   tail   = source[D-X .. D]                         length X
  #   head   = source[0 .. X]                           length X
  #   cf     = xfade(tail, head, offset=0, dur=X)       length X (tail->head transition)
  #   unit   = middle + cf                              length D-X
  # End of unit ≈ start of next unit (both ≈ source[X]) so concat looks seamless.
  CHECK=$(python3 -c "D=${DUR}; X=${XFADE}; print('SHORT' if D <= 2*X else 'OK')")
  if [ "$CHECK" = "SHORT" ]; then
    # Source too short for the requested crossfade; fall back to plain loop
    LOOPED="$WORK_DIR/loop-full-$UUID.mp4"
    ffmpeg -y -stream_loop -1 -i "$SRC" -t "$TARGET" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$LOOPED" 2>/dev/null
    FINAL_RAW="$LOOPED"
    REPEATS=1
    EXTRA_INFO="\"method\":\"crossfade-fallback-loop\",\"xfade_seconds\":$XFADE,\"reason\":\"source shorter than 2x crossfade\""
  else
    MIDDLE_T=$(python3 -c "D=${DUR}; X=${XFADE}; print(f'{D-2*X:.6f}')")
    TAIL_SS=$(python3 -c "D=${DUR}; X=${XFADE}; print(f'{D-X:.6f}')")
    UNIT_LEN=$(python3 -c "D=${DUR}; X=${XFADE}; print(f'{D-X:.6f}')")

    # 1) middle slice  source[X .. D-X]
    MID="$WORK_DIR/loop-mid-$UUID.mp4"
    ffmpeg -y -ss "$XFADE" -i "$SRC" -t "$MIDDLE_T" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$MID" 2>/dev/null

    # 2) tail slice    source[D-X .. D]
    TAIL="$WORK_DIR/loop-tail-$UUID.mp4"
    ffmpeg -y -ss "$TAIL_SS" -i "$SRC" -t "$XFADE" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$TAIL" 2>/dev/null

    # 3) head slice    source[0 .. X]
    HEAD="$WORK_DIR/loop-head-$UUID.mp4"
    ffmpeg -y -i "$SRC" -t "$XFADE" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$HEAD" 2>/dev/null

    # 4) crossfade tail -> head (offset=0, full length is X)
    CF="$WORK_DIR/loop-cf-$UUID.mp4"
    ffmpeg -y -i "$TAIL" -i "$HEAD" -filter_complex "[0:v][1:v]xfade=transition=fade:duration=${XFADE}:offset=0[v]" -map "[v]" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$CF" 2>"$WORK_DIR/xfade-err-$UUID.log" || {
      ERR=$(tail -c 800 "$WORK_DIR/xfade-err-$UUID.log" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))')
      echo "{\"error\":\"crossfade ffmpeg failed\",\"detail\":$ERR}"; exit 0
    }

    # 5) build unit = middle + cf   (length = D - X, end-frame matches next-iteration start-frame)
    UNIT="$WORK_DIR/loop-unit-$UUID.mp4"
    cat > "$WORK_DIR/concat-cf-$UUID.txt" <<EOF
file '$MID'
file '$CF'
EOF
    ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$WORK_DIR/concat-cf-$UUID.txt" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$UNIT" 2>/dev/null

    # 6) stream_loop unit to TARGET
    LOOPED="$WORK_DIR/loop-full-$UUID.mp4"
    ffmpeg -y -stream_loop -1 -i "$UNIT" -t "$TARGET" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -pix_fmt yuv420p -an "$LOOPED" 2>/dev/null

    FINAL_RAW="$LOOPED"
    REPEATS=$(python3 -c "print(int(${TARGET}/${UNIT_LEN}) + 1)")
    EXTRA_INFO="\"method\":\"crossfade\",\"xfade_seconds\":$XFADE,\"unit_duration\":$UNIT_LEN"
  fi
fi

FINAL="$FINAL_RAW"
if [ "$MUTE" = "true" ] || [ "$MUTE" = "True" ] || [ "$MUTE" = "1" ]; then
  MUTED="$WORK_DIR/loop-muted-$UUID.mp4"
  ffmpeg -y -i "$FINAL_RAW" -an -c:v copy "$MUTED" 2>/dev/null
  FINAL="$MUTED"
fi
FD=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$FINAL")
SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$FINAL" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$FINAL")
echo "{\"file\":\"$FINAL\",\"duration\":$FD,\"size_bytes\":$SIZE,\"source_duration\":$DUR,\"repeats\":$REPEATS,$EXTRA_INFO}"

tools.name: stitch_clips
tools.description: Concatenate multiple video clips (URLs or local paths) into a single mp4.

clips.description: Array of video URLs or local paths in order.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
CLIPS="$ARG_CLIPS"
WORK_DIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}/at-fal-stitch-$(python3 -c 'import uuid;print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:8])')"
mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
LIST="$WORK_DIR/list.txt"; : > "$LIST"
IDX=0
while IFS= read -r u; do
  [ -z "$u" ] && continue
  if echo "$u" | grep -qE '^https?://'; then
    F="$WORK_DIR/clip_${IDX}.mp4"; curl -sSL -o "$F" "$u"
  else
    F="$u"
  fi
  echo "file '$F'" >> "$LIST"
  IDX=$((IDX+1))
done < <(echo "$CLIPS" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;[print(u) for u in json.load(sys.stdin)]')
OUT="$WORK_DIR/stitched.mp4"
ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$LIST" -c copy "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || \
  ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$LIST" -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 23 -threads 2 -c:a aac "$OUT" 2>/dev/null
SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUT" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$OUT")
echo "{\"file\":\"$OUT\",\"size_bytes\":$SIZE}"

tools.name: submit_image_to_video
tools.description: Submit an image-to-video job to FAL.ai and return immediately with the request_id. Use this when polling needs to happen separately (e.g. for jobs longer than the bash handler timeout). Pair with check_task or wait_for_video to retrieve the result.

image_url.description: Public HTTPS URL of source image.

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
API_KEY="${VAR_FAL_API_KEY:-${VAR_FAL_KEY:-}}"
if [ -z "$API_KEY" ]; then
  echo '{"error":"FAL API key not configured"}'; exit 0
fi
MODEL="${ARG_MODEL:-fal-ai/minimax/hailuo-02/standard/image-to-video}"
IMG="$ARG_IMAGE_URL"
PROMPT="$ARG_PROMPT"
DUR="${ARG_DURATION:-6}"
AR="${ARG_ASPECT_RATIO:-16:9}"

PAYLOAD=$(python3 -c "
import json,sys
p={'prompt':sys.argv[1],'image_url':sys.argv[2],'duration':sys.argv[3],'aspect_ratio':sys.argv[4]}
print(json.dumps(p))
" "$PROMPT" "$IMG" "$DUR" "$AR")

SUBMIT=$(curl -sS --max-time 30 -X POST "https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL" \
  -H "Authorization: Key $API_KEY" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "$PAYLOAD")

REQ_ID=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('request_id',''))
except: pass")
STATUS_URL=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('status_url',''))
except: pass")
RESP_URL=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | python3 -c "import sys,json
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('response_url',''))
except: pass")

if [ -z "$REQ_ID" ]; then
  ERR=$(echo "$SUBMIT" | head -c 800 | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))')
  echo "{\"error\":\"FAL submit failed\",\"raw\":$ERR}"; exit 0
fi
[ -z "$STATUS_URL" ] && STATUS_URL="https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL/requests/$REQ_ID/status"
[ -z "$RESP_URL" ] && RESP_URL="https://queue.fal.run/$MODEL/requests/$REQ_ID"

echo "{\"request_id\":\"$REQ_ID\",\"model\":\"$MODEL\",\"status_url\":\"$STATUS_URL\",\"response_url\":\"$RESP_URL\",\"status\":\"IN_QUEUE\"}"
